rule: "adminRouter" imports publicProcedure and is a tRPC router — use protectedProcedure for all mutations to prevent unauthorized access.

translations.technical: "adminRouter" imports publicProcedure and is a tRPC router — use protectedProcedure for all mutations to prevent unauthorized access.
translations.plain_language_single: {label} has no login requirement. Anyone who knows the address could access it.
translations.plain_language_multi: {N} parts of your app have no login requirement. Anyone who knows the address could access them.

education.why_it_matters: Login requirements are your app's first line of defense. Without them on sensitive areas, anyone can access functionality intended only for you or your team.
education.what_could_happen: Someone discovers the admin address (through a search engine, guessing, or a shared link) and can add, change, or delete data without any account.
education.what_to_do: Ask your developer to add authentication to this area, or fix it using the guidance in the technical view.

business_impact: Anyone who discovers the URL could access sensitive areas of your app.
consequence: Unauthorized users could change or delete data meant to be private.

affected_nodes.detail: "adminRouter" imports publicProcedure and is a tRPC router — use protectedProcedure for all mutations to prevent unauthorized access.
affected_nodes.plain_language: adminRouter has no login requirement. Anyone who knows the address could access it.

remediation: Replace publicProcedure with protectedProcedure, or add explicit authorization logic.
